change.item: PUBLIC "-//University of California, Los Angeles::Performing Arts Special Collections//TEXT (US::CLU::31::John Vincent Collection)//EN" "Vincent.sgm" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.cdl.xsl (2004-09-16).

did.head: Descriptive Summary

repository.label: Repository
repository.corpname: University of California, Los Angeles. Library. Performing Arts Special Collections

did.abstract: This collection consists of sketches, holographs and transparencies of musical works
did.physloc: Stored off-site at SRLF. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. Please contact the UCLA Performing Arts Special Collections for paging information.

userestrict.head: Publication Rights
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been assigned to the Performing Arts Special Collections. All requests for permission to publish or quote from manuscripts must be submitted in writing to the Librarian for Performing Arts Special Collections. Permission for publication is given on behalf of the Performing Arts Special Collections as the owner of the physical items and is not intended to include or imply permission of the copyright holder(s), which must also be obtained.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: COLLECTION STORED OFF-SITE AT SRLF: Advance notice required for access.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], John Vincent Collection, 31, Performing Arts Special Collections, University of California, Los Angeles.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Vincent was born on May 17, 1902 in Birmingham, AL; diploma from New England Conservatory of Music; BS and MA from George Peabody College; studied with Walter Piston at Harvard Univ., 1933-35, where he received the John Knowles Paine Travelling Fellowship for two years' study with Nadia Boulanger; Ph.D, Cornell Univ., 1942; head of music dept. at Western Kentucky State Univ., 1937-45, then succeeded Arnold Schoenberg as professor of composition at UCLA, 1946-69; conducted orchestras throughout the US and South America; director, Huntington Hartford Foundation, 1952-65; wrote The diatonic modes in modern music (1951); composed many works, including: Three jacks ballet (1941), Primeval void (opera buffa, 1969-71), Symphony in D (1954), Stabat mater (1970), Three Grecian songs, and two string quartets; he died on Jan. 21, 1977 in Santa Monica, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of sketches, holographs and transparencies of musical works by John Nathaniel Vincent. Also includes correspondence, class lecture notes, research materials and notes, programs, UCLA Music Dept. documents, financial and personal records, photographs, commercial and non-commercial disc recordings of music by Vincent, and miscellaneous memorabilia.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
Arranged in the following series:
Biographical materials (boxes 1-3)
Documents relating to musical works (boxes 4,8-10)
Programs, reviews, notes (boxes 5-7)
Articles, speeches, papers (boxes 11-13)
International travels (boxes 14-16)
Correspondence (boxes 17-31,44-47)
Music festivals and performing organizations (boxes 32-43)
UCLA and other academic institutions (boxes 48-56)
Radio broadcast records (boxes 57-59)
Libretti and musical notes (boxes 60-61)
Musical sketches and holographs (boxes 62-72)
Transparent masters of musical works (boxes 73-77)
Notes for the counterpoint and harmony books (boxes 78-80)
The diatonic modes (boxes 81-86)
Financial and personal records (boxes 90-92)
Program books, scrapbooks (boxes 93-95)
Tape recordings of Vincent's music performances, interviews (boxes 96-97)
Memorabilia (boxes 98,101,105)
Miscellaneous (boxes 99-100)
Disc recordings of Vincent's works (boxes 102,104)
Vincent's lectures (box 103)
Music manuscripts (boxes 106-122)
Posters (packages 1-5)

controlaccess.head: Indexing Terms
controlaccess.p: The following terms have been used to index the description of this collection in the library's online public access catalog.
controlaccess.corpname: University of California, Los Angeles--Dept. of Music--Faculty--Archival resources.
controlaccess.subject: Composers--California--Los Angeles--Archival resources.

dsc.head: Collection Contents
dsc.p: A container list for this collection is not yet available online. For detailed information about the contents of this collection contact UCLA Performing Arts Special Collections at the addresses listed on the title page of the finding aid.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Material
relatedmaterial.p: Flautist, composer, teacher [oral history transcript] / John Vincent, interviewee. UCLA Oral History Department interview, c1985. Available at Dept. of Special Collections, UCLA.
